blocks:
Ayahs

قٓ ۚ وَٱلْقُرْءَانِ ٱلْمَجِيدِ (۱)
Qaf. By the glorious Quran. (1)
بَلْ عَجِبُوٓا۟ أَن جَآءَهُم مُّنذِرٌ مِّنْهُمْ فَقَالَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ هَـٰذَا شَىْءٌ عَجِيبٌ (۲)
Indeed they consider it odd that a warner from among themselves should have come to them. So the faithless say, ‘This is an odd thing.’ (2)

Key Points

| This Surah is one of the 29 Surahs that begin with the disjointed letters (Huroof Muqatta'ah). As we have stated repeatedly, these letters indicate the greatness and miraculous nature (I'jaz) of the Quran, which is composed of these very letters of the alphabet. Therefore, immediately following them, an oath is sworn by the Quran. «ق و القرآن المجید» Regarding the Prophet, what was revealed to him, and His commands, two types of astonishment (or wonder) are mentioned in the Quran: one positive and the other negative. The positive astonishment came from a group of Jinn whose high-level content of the Quran caused them to wonder, and they said: «سمعنا قرآنا عجبا»(1) The negative astonishment comes from the disbelievers (Kuffar), which occurs in several instances: From the fact that a person like themselves became a Prophet. «بل عجبوا أن جائهم منذر منهم» From his call to monotheism. «أجعل الالهة الهاً واحداً انّ هذا لشى ء عُجاب»(2) From his speech concerning accountability and the establishment of the Resurrection. «أفمن هذا الحدیث تعجبون»(3) From being brought back to life again. «و ان تَعجب فعجب قولهم أاذا كنّا تُراباً أانّا فى خلق جدید»(4)

1) Al-Jinn, 1. 2) Sad, 5. 3) An-Najm, 59. 4) Ar-Ra'd, 5.

Messages

1- Swearing an oath by the Quran is highly significant because it possesses glory (Majd) and greatness.

«والقرآن المجید»

2- If you seek glory and greatness, turn toward the Quran, which possesses glory and honor. «والقرآن المجید»

3- If the Quran is Glorious (Majid) and Noble (Karim), we must also glorify and honor it.

«و القرآن المجید», «انّه لقرآن كریم»(5)

4- Virtues and perfections appear as objectionable and undesirable in the eyes of the unwise. The fact that the Prophets were of the same kind and came from among the people is a commendable and wise action, but the disbelievers considered it undesirable and were astonished. «عجبوا ان جاءهم منذر منهم»

5- One of the important aspects of the Prophets' mission is warning and admonishing the people. «منذر منهم»

6- Denying Allah's wise action regarding the sending of Prophets is disbelief (Kufr). «عجبوا ان جاءهم... فقال الكافرون»

7- The logic of the disbelievers throughout history has been to find the mission of the Prophets improbable and astonishing. «فقال الكافرون هذا شى ء عجیب» (Indeed, the disbelievers have no proof to negate prophethood or the Resurrection; their only action is astonishment at the warnings of the Prophets, especially concerning the Resurrection.)

5) Al-Waqi'ah, 77.

Explanations

| # Portrait of Surah Qaf This Surah has forty-five verses and was revealed in Mecca. The name of this Surah is derived from its beginning, which is the disjointed letter «ق» (Qaf). Its main focus, like other Meccan Surahs, is the issue of the Resurrection (Ma'ad) and describing the fate of past nations and of good and evil individuals. The Surah begins with the opponents finding the resurrection of man improbable and denying it, and it continues by the Quran citing examples of resurrection found in nature and creation. The manner in which man gives up his soul at the time of death and the shocking events of the end of the world, which are the beginning of the next world, serve as an introduction to describing some of the events of the Resurrection and the characteristics of Paradise and Hellfire. And what protects man from dangers in the midst of all this is the remembrance of Allah and attention to His Word, which is highly recommended in this Surah.

Ayahs

أَءِذَا مِتْنَا وَكُنَّا تُرَابًا ۖ ذَٰلِكَ رَجْعٌۢ بَعِيدٌ (۳)
‘What! When we are dead and have become dust, [shall we be raised again]? That is a far-fetched return!’ (3)
قَدْ عَلِمْنَا مَا تَنقُصُ ٱلْأَرْضُ مِنْهُمْ ۖ وَعِندَنَا كِتَـٰبٌ حَفِيظٌۢ (٤)
We know what the earth diminishes from them, and with Us is a preserving Book. (4)
بَلْ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِٱلْحَقِّ لَمَّا جَآءَهُمْ فَهُمْ فِىٓ أَمْرٍ مَّرِيجٍ (٥)
Indeed, they denied the truth when it came to them; so they are now in a perplexed state of affairs. (5)

Key Points

The intent of the disbelievers regarding raj'un ba'eed (رجع بعید) is its remoteness from the perspective of reason ('aql) and custom ('adah). Meaning, the return of man again is a remote matter from the perspective of reason and custom. Therefore, they have no proof for denying the Resurrection (Ma'ad); they only consider it improbable (istib'ad). Mareej (مریج) means mixing and confusion. Just as the Quran says regarding the mixing of the waters of the two seas: «مَرج البحرَین یلتقیان»(6) The word mareej (مریج) is used only once in the Quran, and it is in the form of the Sifat Mushabbahah (adjective indicating permanence), which signifies the continuation of the bewilderment and confusion of the disbelievers.

6) Ar-Rahman, 19.

Messages

1- Attention to the Resurrection (Ma'ad) and life after death is the central theme of the warnings given by the Prophets. «جاءهم منذر... ذلك رجع بعید»

2- Respond to theological questions and doubts. The astonishment of the disbelievers stems from the disintegration of the human body in the earth, to which the Quran replies: We know this erosion. «أاذا متنا... قد علمنا ما تنقص الارض»

3- All material transformations and changes are calculated and are under the supervision of Allah. «قد علمنا ما تنقص الارض»

4- The earth possesses the property of causing erosion. «تنقص الارض منهم»

5- Allah's knowledge of the particles of existence does not contradict the recording and documentation of affairs. «علمنا... و عندنا كتاب» (It is like when you tell someone: I know it myself, and at the same time, you show them documents and evidence.)

6- Allah completes the argument (Itmam al-Hujjah) even against the disbelievers. «كذّبوا بالحقّ لما جائهم»

7- The root of anxiety and confusion is the denial of the truth (Haqq) and disbelief (Kufr), just as the root of tranquility is attention to the Quran and the remembrance of Allah. «بل كذّبوا بالحقّ... فهم فى أمر مریج»

8- One who lacks firm and stable faith speaks differently every moment and is anxious. (Sometimes they called the Prophet a sorcerer and a soothsayer, and sometimes a madman and a student of others.) «فى أمر مریج»

Ayahs

أَفَلَمْ يَنظُرُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱلسَّمَآءِ فَوْقَهُمْ كَيْفَ بَنَيْنَـٰهَا وَزَيَّنَّـٰهَا وَمَا لَهَا مِن فُرُوجٍ (٦)
Have they not, then, observed the heaven above them, how We have built it and adorned it, and that there are no cracks in it? (6)
وَٱلْأَرْضَ مَدَدْنَـٰهَا وَأَلْقَيْنَا فِيهَا رَوَٰسِىَ وَأَنۢبَتْنَا فِيهَا مِن كُلِّ زَوْجٍۭ بَهِيجٍ (۷)
And We spread out the earth, and cast in it firm mountains, and caused every delightful kind [of vegetation] to grow in it. (7)
تَبْصِرَةً وَذِكْرَىٰ لِكُلِّ عَبْدٍ مُّنِيبٍ (۸)
[In this there is] an insight and admonition for every penitent servant. (8)

Key Points

«Rawaasi» (رواسى) is the plural of «Raasiyah» (راسیة), meaning firm and stable. The statement regarding the pairing (sexuality/duality) of plants is one of the scientific miracles of the Quran. «من كل زوج بهیج»

The root of astonishment and considering the Resurrection (Ma'ad) improbable lies in several things: A) A dead person who has decayed and whose dust has scattered—who knows where it is? B) Assuming the scattered particles are known, by what power will they be gathered?

In response to the first doubt, we read in Ayah 4: We know and recognize all the particles of the earth. «علمنا ما تنقص الارض منهم»

In response to the second doubt, He says in these verses: We have the power to gather those particles, and you observe the example of Our power in the heavens and the earth, how We have raised the heavens and adorned them in such a way that there is no flaw or deficiency in them.

In Islam, reasoning (ta'aqqul), contemplation (tafakkur), insight (basirah), and knowledge (ma'rifah) are the first steps, while supplication (munajat), weeping, repentance (inabah), and worship are the subsequent steps. «تبصرة و ذكرى» (Insight and reminder)—first insight, then remembrance.

Elsewhere, He says: «یتفكّرون فى خلق السّموات والارض ربّنا ما خلقت هذا باطلا سبحانك...»(7) meaning they first contemplate and reason, and then they acknowledge the Lordship of Allah.

The Quran, in praise of the wise, says: «اعینهم تفیض من الدمع مما عرفوا من الحقّ»(8) Their tears arise from thought, knowledge, and insight, not based on habit or emotion.

7) Aal-i-Imran, 191. 8) Al-Ma'idah, 83.

Messages

1- Nature is a classroom for knowing Allah. «أفلم ینظروا الى السّماء...»

2- Invite people by presenting examples. (For the possibility of the Resurrection, look at the display of Allah's power in the heavens and the earth.) «أفلم ینظروا»

3- Those who do not contemplate existence and consider the Resurrection improbable are worthy of blame. «أفلم ینظروا»

4- Order and law govern the celestial spheres. «كیف بنیناها... ما لها من فروج»

5- The earth possesses firm mountains. «ألقینا فیها رواسى»

6- Firmness and beauty are characteristics of Allah's creations in the heavens and the earth. «ما لها من فروج, زیّنا السّماء, القینا... رواسى, زوج بهیج»

7- The growth of living plants from the heart of dead earth is a manifestation of the human resurrection. «ذلك رجع بعید... و انبتنا فیها من كلّ زوج بهیج»

8- Looking at plants and natural scenery is a factor in creating vitality and joy. «افلم ینظروا... من كلّ زوج بهیج»

9- Observe the examples of Allah's power so thoroughly that not a speck of doubt remains. «أفلم ینظروا... بنینا, زیّنا, مددنا, القینا, انبتنا»

10- The cheapest, most universal, closest, healthiest, most durable, most purposeful, and most comprehensive joys are found in observing the Divine creations. «بهیج»

11- In the school of the Prophets, observation must be a prelude to insight, understanding, awareness, and taking heed. «ینظروا - تبصرة - ذكرى»

12- The path of return (repentance) is open to everyone. «لكلّ عبد منیب»

13- If a person possesses a heart capable of understanding, the entire universe (heaven and earth) is a classroom of knowledge for him. «تبصرة و ذكرى لكلّ عبد منیب»

14- In the school of the Prophets, beauties and joys are not means of heedlessness, but rather means of growth and proximity (to Allah). «زیّنا... زوج بهیج... تبصرة و ذكرى لكلّ عبد منیب»

15- Humility is the basis for understanding, while arrogance leads to deprivation. «تبصرة وذكرى لكلّ عبد»

16- Supplication and repentance to Allah are prerequisites of servitude ('ubudiyyah). «عبد منیب»

Ayahs

وَنَزَّلْنَا مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً مُّبَـٰرَكًا فَأَنۢبَتْنَا بِهِۦ جَنَّـٰتٍ وَحَبَّ ٱلْحَصِيدِ (۹)
We send down from the sky salubrious water, with which We grow gardens and the grain which is harvested, (9)
وَٱلنَّخْلَ بَاسِقَـٰتٍ لَّهَا طَلْعٌ نَّضِيدٌ (۱٠)
and tall date palms with regularly set spathes, (10)
رِّزْقًا لِّلْعِبَادِ ۖ وَأَحْيَيْنَا بِهِۦ بَلْدَةً مَّيْتًا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ ٱلْخُرُوجُ (۱۱)
as a provision for Our servants, and with it We revive a dead country. Likewise will be the rising [from the dead]. (11)

Key Points

«حَصید» (Haṣīd) means harvested (or reaped). «باسقات» (Bāsiqāt) means tall-statured (or lofty). «طَلع» (Ṭal‘) is the blossom that has just turned into a date. «نَضید» (Naḍīd) means stacked (or piled up) and compacted. One of the best and most delightful states for the farmer is when the crop ripens and is harvested. The phrase «حبّ الحصید» (grain of the harvest) in these verses is used to explain the Resurrection (Ma'ad), utilizing the descent of rain and the growth of plants. Imam al-Bāqir عليه السلام, regarding «نزلنا من السّماء» (We sent down from the sky), narrated from the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: There is no water on Earth except that it has been mixed with the water of the sky (rain). (9) When it rained, Hadrat Ali عليه السلام would pour the rainwater over His head and face, saying: This is a blessing from the sky that no hand or vessel has touched. (10)

9) Kāfī, Vol. 6, p. 387. 10) Bihār, Vol. 59, p. 270.

Messages

1- Although the falling of rain occurs through natural factors, it is by the Will of Allah. «نزّلنا» (We sent down)

2- Rain is the means of life and blessing for the Earth. The destruction of pests, the growth of plants, the freshness of the air, and the flowing of springs are among the goodness and blessings of rain. «مباركاً» (blessed)

3- Among fruits, the date has a special status. (The date palm tree is mentioned separately) «جنّات... والنخل» (gardens... and the date palms)

4- The emergence of agricultural products is wise and purposeful. «رزقا للعباد» (as provision for the servants)

5- Man's provision (Rizq) should be the basis for his servitude (to Allah). «رزقاً للعباد»

6- To explain abstract concepts (Ma'qūlāt), we should use tangible things (Maḥsūsāt). «احیینا به بلدة میتا... كذلك الخروج» (We revived thereby a dead land... thus will be the emergence/resurrection)

7- A look at the power of Allah makes the issue of reviving the dead easy for man. «بنینا، زینا، مددنا، القینا، أنبتنا، نزلنا، فانبتنا، أحیینا... كذلك الخروج» (We built, We adorned, We spread out, We cast, We caused to grow, We sent down, then We caused to grow, We revived... thus will be the emergence/resurrection)

8- Divine actions possess a special comprehensiveness; Allah has interwoven artistry, wisdom, knowledge, and guidance. (The height of the palm and the beauty of its blossoms are signs of Divine artistry and skill. The fruits being provision is a sign of Divine wisdom, and bringing forth so much greenery and produce from dead earth is the basis for man's guidance and recognition of Divine power over the Resurrection.) «باسقات... نضید... رزقاً... كذلك الخروج»

Ayahs

كَذَّبَتْ قَبْلَهُمْ قَوْمُ نُوحٍ وَأَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلرَّسِّ وَثَمُودُ (۱۲)
The people of Noah denied before them, and [so did] the people of Rass and Thamud, (12)
وَعَادٌ وَفِرْعَوْنُ وَإِخْوَٰنُ لُوطٍ (۱۳)
and ‘Ad, Pharaoh, and the brethren of Lot, (13)
وَأَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْأَيْكَةِ وَقَوْمُ تُبَّعٍ ۚ كُلٌّ كَذَّبَ ٱلرُّسُلَ فَحَقَّ وَعِيدِ (۱٤)
and the inhabitants of Aykah and the people of Tubbac. Each [of them] impugned the apostles, and so My threat became due [against them]. (14)

Key Points

The name "Aṣḥāb al-Rass" (Companions of the Well) is mentioned in this Surah and in Surah Al-Furqan, Ayah 38. The "Aṣḥāb al-Rass" lived in Yamamah and had a prophet named Ḥanẓalah. They denied their prophet and threw him into a well (or pit). Since "Rass" means well (or pit), that people were named Aṣḥāb al-Rass. Some consider them a people who took the pine tree as their deity and worshipped it. (11) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding the reason for the punishment of Aṣḥāb al-Rass: Their women sufficed with women (meaning they were homosexuals). (12) The people of Thamūd were the people of Prophet Ṣāliḥ عليه السلام, and the people of 'Ād were the people of Prophet Hūd عليه السلام. "Aykah" means a thicket or a place where trees are intertwined. The Companions of Aykah were a group from the people of Prophet Shu'ayb who lived in thickets. They are mentioned in Surah Al-Ḥijr and Surah Ash-Shu'arā'. "Tubba'" is the title of the kings of Yemen, and the people of Tubba' refers to a group of people in Yemen who followed their kings. Their name is also mentioned in Surah Ad-Dukhān, Ayah 37. We read in a Ḥadīth that the King of Yemen (Tubba') told the two tribes of Aws and Khazraj: Remain here in Yathrib until the Prophet of Islam is commissioned. And if I were to meet that Prophet, I would serve Him and accompany Him. (13) Since the people of Prophet Lūṭ were all his relatives and kin, the Quran refers to them as "Brothers of Lūṭ" (Ikhwān Lūṭ). (14) Of course, the Quran considers the prophets to be the brothers of the people, and perhaps for this reason, the people of Lūṭ are also referred to as his brothers.

11) Tafsir Namūneh. 12) Kāfī, Vol. 5, p. 551. 13) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayān. 14) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayān.

Messages

1- Familiarity with the history and difficulties faced by the prophets is a source of comfort for the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله. "كُلٌّ كَذَّبَ الرُّسُلَ"

2- The conflict between truth and falsehood has existed throughout history. "كُلٌّ كَذَّبَ الرُّسُلَ"

3- Allah completes the argument (proof) by sending the prophets, and if people persist in obstinacy and refuse to accept, He punishes them. "كَذَّبَ الرُّسُلَ... فَحَقَّ وَعِيدِ"

4- The punishment of some obstinate peoples occurred in this world. "فَحَقَّ وَعِيدِ"

5- The history and destiny of previous nations are a lesson for future generations. "كُلٌّ كَذَّبَ الرُّسُلَ... فَحَقَّ وَعِيدِ"

6- Denying any one of the prophets leads to divine punishment and wrath. "كُلٌّ كَذَّبَ الرُّسُلَ"

Ayahs

أَفَعَيِينَا بِٱلْخَلْقِ ٱلْأَوَّلِ ۚ بَلْ هُمْ فِى لَبْسٍ مِّنْ خَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ (۱٥)
Have We been exhausted by the first creation? No, they are in doubt about a new creation. (15)
وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ وَنَعْلَمُ مَا تُوَسْوِسُ بِهِۦ نَفْسُهُۥ ۖ وَنَحْنُ أَقْرَبُ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ حَبْلِ ٱلْوَرِيدِ (۱٦)
Certainly We have created man and We know to what his soul tempts him, and We are nearer to him than his jugular vein. (16)

Key Points

The word ‘ayīnā (عیینا) is derived from ‘ayy (عى), meaning incapacity and inability. Labs (لَبس) means mixing, error, or becoming ambiguous. Waswasah (وسوسه) literally means a soft sound, and technically refers to undesirable thoughts that pass through a person’s mind and heart. The word warīd (ورید) comes from the root wurūd (ورود), meaning to approach water. Since this vein is the main channel for blood circulation between the heart and other parts of the body, it is called warīd (jugular vein).

To guide the misguided, every correct method must be utilized. In the previous verses, examples of Allah’s power in existence and examples of the grim fate of those who denied the truth were presented. These verses address the conscience and intellect of the people, asking whether We were incapacitated by the first creation that We should fail at the second creation.

Similar to the phrase «نحن أقرب الیه من حبل الورید» (We are nearer to him than the jugular vein), is this verse: «انّ اللّه یحول بین المرء و قلبه»(15) (Indeed, Allah intervenes between a man and his heart).

The source of waswasah (whispering/temptation) is threefold: a) The self and carnal desires. «توسوس به نفسه» b) Satan. «فوسوس لهما الشیطان»(16) c) Some humans and jinn. «الّذى یوسوس فى صدور النّاس من الجِنّة والناس»(17)

Allah’s knowledge of our affairs is due to two reasons: a) Because He is our Creator, and the Creator knows His creation: «الا یعلم من خلق»(18), «و لقد خلقنا الانسان و نعلم ما توسوس به نفسه» (And certainly We created man, and We know what his soul whispers to him). b) Because He encompasses us. «نعلم... و نحن أقرب الیه من حبل الورید» Therefore, God’s knowledge is precise, vast, and direct.

The root of doubt concerning the Resurrection (Ma‘ad) is twofold: First, how does God know what everyone has done and where their particles are? Second, how can He recreate them? This verse answers both: He can, because He created man before. «ولقد خلقنا الانسان» He knows, because He even knows the unseen whisperings. «نعلم ما توسوس به نفسه»

15) Al-Anfal, 24. 16) Al-A‘raf, 20. 17) An-Nas, 5 and 6. 18) Al-Mulk, 14.

Messages

  1. The creation of any being is proof of the Creator’s power to recreate it. If the first creation was possible, the second creation is also possible. «افعیینا بالخلق الاول»
  2. When speaking with opponents, use the shortest and most eloquent words. «افعیینا بالخلق الاول» (Considering one possible and the other impossible is a contradiction.)
  3. Disbelievers have no proof for denying the Resurrection; it is only doubt and confusion. «بل هم فى لبس من خلق جدید»
  4. If you wish to have a sound belief, you must distance yourself from whisperings and reform your soul. (The denial of the Resurrection is because you have not purified yourself from whisperings.) «بل هم فى لبس... و نعلم ما توسوس به نفسه»
  5. Allah’s knowledge and encompassment of humanity spans his entire life. «نحن أقرب الیه من حبل الورید»

Ayahs

إِذْ يَتَلَقَّى ٱلْمُتَلَقِّيَانِ عَنِ ٱلْيَمِينِ وَعَنِ ٱلشِّمَالِ قَعِيدٌ (۱۷)
When the twin recorders record [his deeds], seated on the right hand and on the left: (17)
مَّا يَلْفِظُ مِن قَوْلٍ إِلَّا لَدَيْهِ رَقِيبٌ عَتِيدٌ (۱۸)
he says no word but that there is a ready observer beside him. (18)
وَجَآءَتْ سَكْرَةُ ٱلْمَوْتِ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۖ ذَٰلِكَ مَا كُنتَ مِنْهُ تَحِيدُ (۱۹)
The throes of death bring the truth: ‘This is what you used to shun!’ (19)
وَنُفِخَ فِى ٱلصُّورِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ يَوْمُ ٱلْوَعِيدِ (۲٠)
And the Trumpet will be blown: ‘This is the promised day.’ (20)

Key Points

The expression qa'īd (قعید) is a metaphor indicating that the two angels are always with man, lying in wait for him, not that the two angels are literally sitting on the right and left sides of man. Raqīb (رقیب) means observer and supervisor, Atīd (عتید) means ready for service, Sakrah (سكرة) is a state where consciousness and intellect depart from man, and tahīd (تحید) means to deviate or flee.

The terror and anxiety of man at the time of death are so intense that the Quran refers to it as sakrat al-mawt (the stupor of death). Similarly, on the Day of Judgment, the horror of the Divine court causes man to enter such a state that, in the words of the Quran: «تَرى النّاس سُكارى »(19) (You see people as if they are drunk, their minds gone and without will).

Among the organs of the body, the surveillance of the tongue is specifically mentioned, perhaps because the action of the tongue is more frequent, easier, more common, deeper, more continuous, and more prone to incident than all other organs.

Paying attention to the fact that man is under surveillance, that all his deeds are recorded, and focusing on the moment of death and the presence on the Day of Judgment, is a great warning and prepares the ground for Divine piety.

Attention to the multiplicity of observers and witnesses affects man's performance, and the more witnesses and observers there are, the greater the motivation for good deeds and avoidance of sin. «عن الیمین... عن الشّمال... رقیب عتید»

19) Hajj, 2.

Messages

1- Although Allah the Almighty Himself is a witness to man's deeds and thoughts, the Divine administration maintains accountability, and angels are constantly engaged in recording deeds. «اذ یتلقّى المتلقّیان»

2- There is a division of labor among the angels. (Based on narrations, the angel of the right records good deeds, and the angel of the left records bad and evil deeds.(20)) «المتلقیان عن الیمین و عن الشّمال قعید»

3- Man is accountable and will be judged not only for his actions but also for his speech. «ما یلفظ من قول»

4- Surveillance that is valid and valuable is that which completely oversees everything. «عن الیمین... عن الشمال... رقیب عتید»

5- Man loses his normal state at the moment of death. «سكرة الموت»

6- The stupor of death is inevitable for everyone. «سكرة الموت بالحق»

7- Fleeing from death is a natural characteristic of man. «كنت منه تحید» 20) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Ayahs

وَجَآءَتْ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَّعَهَا سَآئِقٌ وَشَهِيدٌ (۲۱)
Every soul will come accompanied by [two angels], a driver, and a witness: (21)
لَّقَدْ كُنتَ فِى غَفْلَةٍ مِّنْ هَـٰذَا فَكَشَفْنَا عَنكَ غِطَآءَكَ فَبَصَرُكَ ٱلْيَوْمَ حَدِيدٌ (۲۲)
[he will be told] ‘You were certainly oblivious of this. We have removed your veil from you, and so today your eyesight is acute.’ (22)
وَقَالَ قَرِينُهُۥ هَـٰذَا مَا لَدَىَّ عَتِيدٌ (۲۳)
Then his companion [angel] will say, ‘This is what is ready with me [of his record of deeds].’ (23)
أَلْقِيَا فِى جَهَنَّمَ كُلَّ كَفَّارٍ عَنِيدٍ (۲٤)
[The two angels accompany him will be told,] ‘Cast every obdurate ingrate into hell, (24)
مَّنَّاعٍ لِّلْخَيْرِ مُعْتَدٍ مُّرِيبٍ (۲٥)
[every] hinderer of good, transgressor, and skeptic, (25)
ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَـٰهًا ءَاخَرَ فَأَلْقِيَاهُ فِى ٱلْعَذَابِ ٱلشَّدِيدِ (۲٦)
who had set up another god along with Allah and cast him into the severe punishment.’ (26)

Key Points

In the Hereafter (Qiyamah), the natural stages of a court proceeding are followed until the verdict is issued: first, the presence of the witness ("سائق و شهید" - the driver and the witness), followed by the trial and the notification of the crime, "كنت فى غفلة" (You were in heedlessness), and then the issuance of the verdict. "القیا فى جهنّم" (Cast [him] into Hell).

"عتید" (ready/present) refers to the precise order and control of the angels, and "عنید" (obstinate) means stubborn and defiant. "مَنّاع" (preventer) derived from man' (to prevent), signifies stinginess, expressing the severity of avarice and prevention. "مُریب" (doubter/suspicious) refers to someone who casts others into doubt, specifically a doubt accompanied by pessimism.

The word "كَفّار" (intensely disbelieving) expresses the peak and depth of disbelief (kufr) in a human being, which naturally indicates severe obstinacy and defiance. This disbelief prevents him from everything [good], leads him to oppression and transgression, and also creates doubt in others regarding the truthfulness of Allah's path. It is natural that the end result for such an individual is Hellfire and severe torment.

People fall into several groups regarding good deeds: One group performs good deeds and strives to precede others in them. "فاستبقوا الخیرات" (21) Another group is a source of good and invites others to it. "یدعون الى الخیر" (22) But a third group severely prevents good. "منّاع للخیر" (A severe preventer of good).

There is proportionality between human crime and Divine punishment. Whoever commits disbelief (kufr), obstinacy, prevention of good, transgression, casting doubt, and polytheism (shirk), his torment must be severe. "فى العذاب الشدید" (In the severe torment).

Imam Baqir and Imam Sadiq علیهما السلام said: The meaning of "قرینه" (companion) is the angel who is a witness over him. (23) The two angels assigned to cast the criminals into Hell are either Raqib and Atid, or Sa'iq and Shahid.

21) Baqarah, 148. 22) Aal-i-Imran, 104. 23) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

  1. Every human being will be present on the Day of Judgment, and just like a worldly court, he will be accompanied by a vigilant angel who keeps him under observation. "و جاءت كل نفس معها سائق"

  2. On the Day of Judgment, there is neither the ability to escape nor the ability to deny. "معها سائق و شهید"

  3. The duty and responsibility of every angel are specified. "سائق و شهید"

  4. Human heedlessness (ghafleh) is deep and pervasive. "فى غفلةٍ" (The tanwin [nunation] indicates importance, and the context of "فى" [in] indicates its encompassing and pervasive nature.)

  5. The world and its manifestations cast a veil of heedlessness over man. "غفلة... غطاءك"

  6. Man creates veils of heedlessness for himself through his own actions. "غطاءك"

  7. The world is the abode of heedlessness, and the Hereafter is the realm of awareness. "كنت فى غفلة... فبصرك الیوم حدید"

  8. The veils that man creates for himself through his behavior become a barrier to his perception of truth, and the day the veils are removed, his vision becomes real. "غطاءك... فبصرك الیوم حدید"

  9. The heedless person is superficial in the world and lacks deep insight, but on the Day of Judgment, his eyes are opened and become sharp-sighted. "فى غفلة... الیوم حدید"

  10. The command to cast [them] into Hell expresses the humiliation and abasement of the inhabitants of the Fire. "القیا"

  11. The criterion on the Day of Judgment is human performance, regardless of his gender, race, or tribe. "كلّ كَفّار"

  12. What causes entry into Hell is stubborn and defiant disbelief (kufr) and denial, not ignorant disbelief. "كَفّار عنید"

  13. Disbelief (kufr) and polytheism (shirk) are the source of committing corruption. "كفّار... منّاع للخیر معتد مریب الّذى جعل مع اللّه"

  14. The rulings of the Divine Court are so firm and wise that they are not subject to appeal. "القیا فى جهنّم كلّ كفّار عنید... فالقیاه فى العذاب الشدید"

  15. Hell has various levels (darakaat) and degrees of torment. "القیا فى جهنّم... فالقیاه فى العذاب الشدید"

Ayahs

۞ قَالَ قَرِينُهُۥ رَبَّنَا مَآ أَطْغَيْتُهُۥ وَلَـٰكِن كَانَ فِى ضَلَـٰلٍۭ بَعِيدٍ (۲۷)
His companion [devil] will say, ‘Our Lord! I did not incite him to rebel [against You], but he was [himself] in extreme error.’ (27)
قَالَ لَا تَخْتَصِمُوا۟ لَدَىَّ وَقَدْ قَدَّمْتُ إِلَيْكُم بِٱلْوَعِيدِ (۲۸)
He will say, ‘Do not wrangle in My presence, for I had already warned you in advance. (28)
مَا يُبَدَّلُ ٱلْقَوْلُ لَدَىَّ وَمَآ أَنَا۠ بِظَلَّـٰمٍ لِّلْعَبِيدِ (۲۹)
The word [of judgement] is unalterable with Me, and I am not tyrannical to My servants.’ (29)
يَوْمَ نَقُولُ لِجَهَنَّمَ هَلِ ٱمْتَلَأْتِ وَتَقُولُ هَلْ مِن مَّزِيدٍ (۳٠)
The day when We shall say to hell, ‘Are you full?’ It will say, ‘Is there any more?’ (30)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of al-Qarīn (the companion) in this Ayah is Satan. (24) Yes, we also read elsewhere that Satan becomes the companion (qarīn) of some transgressors. (25) "فهو له قرين" (26) One of the factors leading a person to Hellfire is a bad friend or companion, just as the Quran, in these verses, names Satan as the companion who casts man into Hellfire. One of the lamentations of the inhabitants of Hell on the Day of Judgment is expressing regret for having taken bad friends in the world, as the Quran states their words thus: "يا ويلتى ليتنى لم أتّخذ فلاناً خليلاً" (27) Woe is me! I wish I had not taken so-and-so as a friend. On the Day of Judgment, there will be dialogues between the criminals themselves, between the criminals and their corrupt leaders, and between the criminals and Satan, and each group will try to place the blame for their sin upon the other. Scenes of these disputes are narrated in the Quran, including: Sometimes they say to one another: "لولا أنتم لكنّا مؤمنين" (28) If it were not for you, we would certainly have been believers. But they receive the reply: "بل لم تكونوا مؤمنين" (29) Never so; rather, you yourselves were not believers. In another scene and position, they say to the corrupt leaders: "كنّا لكم تبعاً فهل أنتم مغنون عنّا" (30) We were followers of yours and became wretched; can you today solve any problem for us? And in one scene, they blame Satan, and he says: "لاتلومونى و لوموا أنفسكم..." (31) Do not blame me, but blame yourselves. I had nothing to do but invite you; you yourselves accepted my invitation. This Ayah is also a statement from Iblis, who says: My Lord, I did not lead them to transgression. Hell constantly says: "هل من مزيد" (Is there any more?), just as the inhabitants of Hell, in their worldliness, wealth, status, and consumption of people's property, were never satisfied and used to say, "هل من مزيد."

24) Bihar, vol. 5, p. 323. 25) Tafsir al-Mizan. 26) Al-Zukhruf, 36. 27) Al-Furqan, 28. 28) Saba, 31. 29) Al-Saffat, 29. 30) Ibrahim, 21. 31) Ibrahim, 22.

Messages

  1. Where a bad friend and companion leads a person; a person who could have been a companion of the righteous has become a companion of Satan. "قرينه"
  2. Iblis acknowledges the Lordship of Allah. "قال قرينه ربّنا"
  3. Let us be aware that Iblis will disclaim responsibility on the Day of Judgment and dissociate himself from the deviated ones. "ما اطغیته"
  4. Iblis considers his work to be limited only to invitation and temptation, not compulsion of people. "ما اطغیته"
  5. Man's condition reaches a point where Satan considers him misguided. "قال قرينه... فى ضلال بعيد"
  6. The inhabitants of Hell will have disputes and conflicts with Iblis on the Day of Judgment. "لاتختصموا لدىّ"
  7. Instead of disputing and conflicting with Satan on the Day of Judgment, which is useless, we should rise up to fight him in this world. "لاتختصموا لدىّ"
  8. Allah's wrath comes after the completion of the argument (proof). "القیا فى جهنّم... قد قَدّمتُ الیكم بالوعید"
  9. Dialogues and arguments on the Day of Judgment do not change the Divine decree and do not cause a review or reconsideration of the judgment. "ما یبدّل القول لدىّ"
  10. Changing the decree or reducing the punishment of the oppressor would result in oppression against the oppressed. "ما یبدّل القول لدىّ و ما انا بظلاّم للعبید"
  11. The casting of criminals into Hell is an injustice they committed against themselves, not an injustice committed by Allah against them. "القیا فى جهنّم... و ما انا بظلام للعبید"
  12. Hell possesses a kind of consciousness, is addressed by Allah, and answers the Divine question. "هل امتلأت فتقول هل من مزید"

Ayahs

وَأُزْلِفَتِ ٱلْجَنَّةُ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ غَيْرَ بَعِيدٍ (۳۱)
And paradise will be brought near for the Godwary, it will not be distant [any more]: (31)
هَـٰذَا مَا تُوعَدُونَ لِكُلِّ أَوَّابٍ حَفِيظٍ (۳۲)
‘This is what you were promised. [It is] for every penitent and dutiful [servant] (32)
مَّنْ خَشِىَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنَ بِٱلْغَيْبِ وَجَآءَ بِقَلْبٍ مُّنِيبٍ (۳۳)
who fears the All-beneficent in secret and comes with a penitent heart. (33)

Key Points

Khashyah (reverential fear) refers to the fear and dread that arises from the knowledge and majesty of Allah.

The desired hospitality is that the table (of food) is brought to the guest, not the guest brought to the table. Just as Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) brought the table to the guests, «فقرّبه الیهم» (32).

Similarly, on the Day of Judgment, Paradise will be brought near the righteous so that there is no need to traverse a distance. «ازلفت الجنة للمتّقین» The inhabitants of Hell are cast into Hell, «القیا فى جهنّم» (33), but the inhabitants of Paradise are honored, and Paradise is brought near to them. «اُزلفت الجنة»

The inhabitants of Hell are given the threat of punishment and are warned, «قَدّمتُ الیكم بالوعید» (34), but the inhabitants of Paradise are given good tidings and glad news. «هذا ما توعدون»

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said to Ibn Mas'ud: O son of Mas'ud! Fear Allah in secret as if you see Him, and if you do not see Him, He surely sees you. Then He recited this Ayah: «مَن خشى الرّحمن بالغیب...» (35)

The Qur'an speaks of three types of hearts: a) The sound heart (Qalb Salim), «الاّ مَن أتى اللّه بقلب سلیم» (36), which is safe from polytheism, hypocrisy, malice, and other defects. b) The repentant heart (Qalb Munib), «و جاء بقلب منیب» (37), which, after a slip, repents and turns back to Allah. c) The sick heart (Qalb Marid), «فى قلوبهم مرض» (38), which contains hypocrisy and disbelief regarding Divine knowledge, and if left unchecked, its sickness spreads day by day.

32) Adh-Dhariyat, 27. 33) Qaf, 24. 34) Qaf, 28. 35) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 74, p. 111. 36) Ash-Shu'ara, 89. 37) Qaf, 33. 38) Al-Baqarah, 10.

Messages

1- In education and training, encouragement must accompany warning. «القیا فى جهنّم... ازلفت الجنة»

2- Chastity and piety bring Paradise near to man. «اُزلفت الجنّة للمتّقین»

3- Criteria and actions are important, not the gender of individuals. «كلّ كفّار... كلّ أوّاب»

4- Encourage sinners to repent. «هذا ما توعدون لكلّ أوّاب»

5- The path to receiving Paradise is returning to the way of Allah and remaining on that path. «هذا ما توعدون لكلّ أوّاب»

6- The non-infallible human being falls into sin; what is important is the return and repentance. «لكلّ اوّاب»

7- Divine Mercy should not lead to arrogance; rather, one must always maintain concern and reverential fear (khashyah). (Even though Allah is the Most Merciful of the Merciful, one must maintain humility and khashyah due to the majesty of His station.) «خشى الرّحمن»

8- If we maintain boundaries only out of fear of penalty, disgrace, public criticism, reduced wages, etc., it holds little value. Fear of Allah in secret is a sign of khashyah. «خشى الرّحمن بالغیب»

9- The Muttaqi (pious person) is not exclusively someone who never sins, but also someone who, if they commit a slip, seriously repents and possesses a repentant heart and soul (Qalb Munib). «للمتّقین... لكلّ اوّاب... خشى... بقلب منیب»

10- The source of turning back (repentance) to Allah is the knowledge of the Divine station and khashyah (reverential fear) of Him. «خشى الرّحمن... و جاء بقلب منیب»

Ayahs

ٱدْخُلُوهَا بِسَلَـٰمٍ ۖ ذَٰلِكَ يَوْمُ ٱلْخُلُودِ (۳٤)
Enter it in peace! This is the day of immortality.’ (34)
لَهُم مَّا يَشَآءُونَ فِيهَا وَلَدَيْنَا مَزِيدٌ (۳٥)
There they will have whatever they wish, and with Us there is yet more. (35)

Key Points

In the previous verses, four perfections were mentioned for the righteous (Muttaqin): 'Awwāb (the repentant), Hafīẓ (the guardian of himself and the divine limits), Khashyah (fear of God), and Qalb Munīb (possessing a humble and submissive heart). In this verse, four characteristics are stated for Paradise: Salām (security and safety), Khulūd (eternity and permanence), Mā Yashā'ūn (variety and fulfillment), and Ladainā Mazīd (vastness and abundance of blessings).

The blessings of this world usually possess defects that are absent in Paradise, including: 1. They are accompanied by trouble and suffering, but in Paradise, there is no worry or suffering whatsoever. (Bi-Salām) 2. They are temporary, but Paradise is permanent. (Yawm al-Khulūd) 3. Their type is limited, but in Paradise, there is whatever they desire. (Mā Yashā'ūn) 4. Their quantity is limited, but in Paradise, it is ever-increasing. (Wa Ladainā Mazīd)

Messages

1- Fear and awe (Khashyah) of Allah in this world lead to security in the Hereafter. «خشى الرّحمن... ادخلوها بسلام»

2- The inhabitants of the Fire are cast into Hell with humiliation, «القیا فى جهنّم»(39) but the inhabitants of Paradise are welcomed and greeted. «أدخلوها بسلام»

3- Allah possesses both wrath, «القیا فى جهنّم», and mercy. «أدخلوها بسلام»

4- The inhabitants of the Fire are commanded not to speak in the presence of Allah, «لاتختصموا لدىّ», but Allah and the angels greet the people of Paradise. «أدخلوها بسلام», «سلام قولاً من ربّ رحیم»(40)

5- Among the best tidings is the good news of the permanence and imperishability of the blessings in Paradise. «یوم الخلود»

6- Whoever foregoes desires in this world will attain his hopes and wishes in Paradise. «للمتّقین... لهم ما یشاؤن»

7- Regarding the inhabitants of the Fire, Allah deals with His justice, «و ما أنا بظلام للعبید»(41), but regarding the inhabitants of Paradise, He acts with His grace. «و لدینا مزید»

8- Man is a being who seeks the infinite, and only the infinite can satisfy him. «و لدینا مزید»

9- Allah's favor upon the inhabitants of Paradise is beyond their wishes and expectations. «لهم ما یشاؤن فیها و لدینا مزید»(39) Qaf, 24. (40) Ya Sin, 58. (41) Qaf, 29.

Ayahs

وَكَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا قَبْلَهُم مِّن قَرْنٍ هُمْ أَشَدُّ مِنْهُم بَطْشًا فَنَقَّبُوا۟ فِى ٱلْبِلَـٰدِ هَلْ مِن مَّحِيصٍ (۳٦)
How many generations We have destroyed before them, who were stronger than these, insomuch that they ransacked the towns?! So, is there any escape [from Allah’s punishment]? (36)
إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَذِكْرَىٰ لِمَن كَانَ لَهُۥ قَلْبٌ أَوْ أَلْقَى ٱلسَّمْعَ وَهُوَ شَهِيدٌ (۳۷)
There is indeed an admonition in that for one who has a heart, or gives ear, being attentive. (37)

Key Points

Qarn (قَرن) means the proximity of two things to one another. People who live in the same time period are called qarn. Subsequently, this term has been applied to the time period itself (one hundred or thirty years). Batsh (بَطش) means seizing something with power, and sometimes it refers to war and conflict. Naqqibū (نَقِّبوا) is derived from naqb (نقب), meaning to create a hole, a fissure, or a path of penetration in a wall. The word manqabah (مَنقبت) comes from the same root, referring to perfections that penetrate the souls of people. The intent of the Ayah is that some past nations engaged in conquest by penetrating other nations, carving out paths, and advancing. The meaning of qalb (heart) is not the pine-cone shaped organ, but rather the center of human perception. Imam Kadhim عليه السلام says: The intent of qalb is the intellect (aql). (42) We read a similar Ayah in Surah Al-Mulk: «لو كنّا نسمع أو نعقل ما كنّا فى أصحاب السعیر» (43) If we had listened to the truth or used our intellect, we would not be among the inhabitants of the Blaze today. In that Ayah, intellect is mentioned alongside hearing, and in the Ayah under discussion, the heart (qalb) is mentioned alongside hearing; therefore, the intent of qalb is the same as the intellect (aql). Ilqā’ al-sam‘ (القاى سمع) is a metaphor for listening and hearing with the intention of learning; in other words, the intent is the ear of the soul, not the physical ear. Shahīd (شهید) means witness and present. Meaning, someone who is present in the gathering and listens carefully to the words, not someone whose ear, so to speak, is here, but his mind is elsewhere.

(42) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Al-Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 16. (43) Al-Mulk, 10.

Messages

1- The destruction of past nations and civilizations due to disbelief (kufr) and polytheism (shirk) is a guiding light for future generations. «و كم أهلكنا»

2- The destruction of powerful transgressors is a source of comfort and hope for the people of faith. «و كم أهلكنا»

3- The destruction of powerful opponents of the truth is a Divine tradition (Sunnah). «و كم أهلكنا»

4- The study of history and archaeology must lead to the recognition of Divine traditions in history and the discovery of Divine messages. «و كم أهلكنا»

5- Material powers are insignificant when faced with Divine wrath. «أهلكنا... أشدّ... بطشا»

6- The disbelievers of Quraysh considered themselves powerful; therefore, Allah says: We have destroyed those who were mightier than them. «أشدّ منهم بطشاً»

7- Power devoid of faith leads to rebellion, aggression against others, and conquest. «أشدّ بطشاً فنقّبوا فى البلاد»

8- At the time of Divine wrath, all paths of salvation reach a dead end, even for the most powerful individuals. «هل من محیص»

9- Those who take heed from history are those who are either inwardly receptive, «لذكرى لمن كان له قلب», or who hear and accept the truth externally. «ألقى السّمع»

10- We should not pass over historical events lightly; we must analyze them and learn lessons. Those who do not take lessons from events have dead hearts. «لذكرى لمن كان له قلب أو القى السّمع و هو شهید»

11- Hearing and listening are effective only when accompanied by attention and presence of heart. «و هو شهید»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا فِى سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ وَمَا مَسَّنَا مِن لُّغُوبٍ (۳۸)
Certainly We created the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them, in six days, and any fatigue did not touch Us. (38)

Key Points

Lughūb (لُغوب) means toil and fatigue, which is meaningful concerning limited human powers. However, it has no meaning concerning Allah, whose power is limitless. Is He who is able to create the heavens and the earth easily incapable of recreating us?

The intent of yawm (یوم) in the Ayah is not a day or a 24-hour period; perhaps the intention is six stages or eras, because the day in the conventional sense appeared after the creation of the heavens and the earth.

Fakhr al-Razi considers this Ayah a response to the belief of the Jews who say: Allah began creation on Sunday, finished it on Friday, and rested on Saturday!

Allah's creation has specific characteristics: 1. It is novel and innovative. «بدیع السّموات و الارض» (44) 2. It is purposeful. «ما خلقت هذا باطلا» (45) 3. It is undergoing change, transformation, and expansion. «و انّا لموسعون» (46) 4. It is planned and measured. «كل شى ء خلقناه بقدر» (47) 5. Everything is perfect in its own right. «احسن كل شى ء خلقه» (48) 6. It is gradual and stage-by-stage. «خلقنا... فى ستّة ایّام»

In Ayah 15, we read: Were We exhausted by the first creation? «أفعیینا بالخلق الاول» And in this Ayah, He says: Not an atom of fatigue reached Us. «و ما مسّنا من لغوب»

44) بقره، ۱۱۷. 45) آل عمران، ۱۹۱. 46) ذاریات، ۴۷. 47) قمر، ۴۹. 48) سجده، ۷.

Messages

1- Let us not view the heaven simply, for, in the words of the Quran, it is the place where Allah displays His power, possessing vast expanse and multiple layers. «السموات»

2- There are secrets and wisdoms in the gradual creation of beings; otherwise, weakness, fatigue, or defect have no place in the infinite power of Allah, and He could create everything with a single will and in a single moment. «خلقنا... فى ستّة ایّام»

3- Fatigue has no meaning for the absolute Divine power. «ما مسّنا من لغوب»

Ayahs

فَٱصْبِرْ عَلَىٰ مَا يَقُولُونَ وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ قَبْلَ طُلُوعِ ٱلشَّمْسِ وَقَبْلَ ٱلْغُرُوبِ (۳۹)
So be patient at what they say, and celebrate the praise of your Lord before the rising of the sun and before the sunset, (39)
وَمِنَ ٱلَّيْلِ فَسَبِّحْهُ وَأَدْبَـٰرَ ٱلسُّجُودِ (٤٠)
and glorify Him through part of the night and after the prostrations. (40)

Key Points

Islam has commanded the glorification (Tasbih) of Allah in both joy and sorrow. In joy: «اذا جاء نصراللّه والفتح... فسبّح بحمد ربّك...»(49) In sorrow: «فاصبر على ما یقولون و سبح بحمد ربّك...» A verse similar to these two is found in Surah Taha, Ayah 130: «فاصبر على ما یقولون و سبّح بحمد ربّك قبل طلوع الشّمس و قبل غروبها و من اناء الّیل فسبّح و أطراف النّهار» The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله sometimes felt distressed by the vain words of the polytheists. «و لقد نعلم انك یضیق صدرك بما یقولون»(50) Therefore, Allah explicitly commanded him: «فاصبر على ما یقولون» Some of the false beliefs and improper statements of the polytheists are narrated in the Quran, including: - They accepted a son and partners for Allah and considered idols to be intermediaries of grace and intercessors. - They used terms like sorcerer, soothsayer, poet, protégé, and madman regarding the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. Therefore, in these verses, Allah has commanded patience and has counted the remembrance (Dhikr) of God as the way to stand firm against opponents.

In the Quran, glorification (Tasbih) is usually accompanied by divine praise (Hamd), which perhaps points to the fact that when a person sees himself as imperfect and God as perfect, he glorifies Him (Tasbih); and when Allah transforms his imperfection into perfection, he praises Him (Hamd). «سبّح بحمد ربّك»

Out of the eighteen times that God commands the Prophet to be patient, four times relate to patience against vain speech, which are mentioned in Surahs Taha Ayah 130, Sad Ayah 17, Muzzammil Ayah 10, and Qaf Ayah (39).

According to Ibn Abbas, the meaning of "before sunrise" is the Morning Prayer (Salat al-Subh), and "before sunset" refers to the Noon and Afternoon Prayers (Salat al-Zuhr and al-Asr). The intent of «من الّیل» is the Sunset and Evening Prayers (Salat al-Maghrib and al-Isha), and the meaning of «ادبار السّجود» is the supererogatory prayers (Nawafil).(51)

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The meaning of «ادبار السجود» is the two cycles (rak'ahs) of supererogatory prayer after the Sunset Prayer (Maghrib).(52)

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, regarding the verse «و سبّح بحمد ربّك قبل طلوع الشّمس و قبل غروبها», said: Say ten times in the morning and evening: «لا اله الاّ اللّه وحده لا شریك له و له الملك و له الحمد یحیى و یمیت و هو على كلّ شى ء قدیر».(53)

The times for worship in Islam are based on natural signs, such as the states of the moon and the sun, which are accessible to everyone. «قبل طلوع الشّمس و قبل غروبها»

49) Nasr, 1 - 3. 50) Hijr, 97. 51) Tafsir Maraghi. 52) Wasa'il, Vol. 4, p. 73. 53) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Attention to the power of Allah leads to patience and steadfastness against hardships. «لقد خلقنا السّموات و الارض... فاصبر...»

2- Guidance, propagation, and teaching require patience and perseverance. «فاصبر»

3- Prophets advance the path of guidance through divine reminder and command. «فاصبر»

4- Patience against verbal abuse and taunts is difficult even for the Prophet; therefore, Allah recommends it. «فاصبر على ما یقولون»

5- The factor and support for patience is the remembrance of God. «فاصبر... و سبّح بحمد ربّك»

6- The glorification of Allah must be accompanied by His praise. Exonerating Him from defects is not enough; praise and commendation are also necessary. «و سبّح بحمد ربّك»

7- The Lordship (Rububiyyah) of God is the motivation for our praise, and our praise is the factor for our growth. «بحمد ربّك»

8- The glorification and exoneration of Allah precedes His praise and commendation. «و سبّح بحمد ربّك»

9- For worship and supplication, certain times have priority. «قبل طلوع الشّمس و...»

10- For spiritual perfection, man needs repetition and continuity in worship. «قبل طلوع الشّمس و قبل الغروب . و من الّیل»

11- The followers of the Prophet dedicate a portion of the night to worship. «و من الّیل فسبّحه»

12- In addition to obligatory prayers and remembrances (Adhkar), supererogatory prayers and remembrances are also commanded by Allah. «و ادبار السّجود»

Ayahs

وَٱسْتَمِعْ يَوْمَ يُنَادِ ٱلْمُنَادِ مِن مَّكَانٍ قَرِيبٍ (٤۱)
And be on the alert for the day when the caller calls from a close quarter, (41)
يَوْمَ يَسْمَعُونَ ٱلصَّيْحَةَ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ يَوْمُ ٱلْخُرُوجِ (٤۲)
the day when they hear the Cry in all truth. That is the day of rising [from the dead]. (42)

Key Points

In Ayah 11, the principle of the dead emerging from the graves is stated, and in Ayah 42, the time of their emergence is specified. The expression "a place nearby" (مكان قریب) indicates that this sound will spread throughout the atmosphere such that it will seem as if it is right next to everyone's ear, and everyone will hear it equally and from close proximity. On that Day, without the need for any special device, all the inhabitants of the gathering place (Mahshar) will hear the voice of the caller near themselves. The intent of the "Cry" (صیحه) in these verses is not the first Cry which announces the end of this world, but rather the second Cry, which is the Cry of the rising and gathering of the people (Hashr).

Messages

1- Remembering the Day of Judgment and the punishment of the blasphemous disbelievers grants man patience and steadfastness. «فاصبر على ما یقولون... و استمع یوم یناد...»

2- The heavenly Cry (the Trumpet of Israfil) on the threshold of the Day of Judgment is a definite and rightful matter (not merely ceremonial). «الصیحة بالحق»

3- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is physical, and humans will emerge from this very earth. «یوم الخروج», just as we read in the subsequent verses: «تشقّق الارض عنهم»

Ayahs

إِنَّا نَحْنُ نُحْىِۦ وَنُمِيتُ وَإِلَيْنَا ٱلْمَصِيرُ (٤۳)
Indeed it is We who give life and bring death, and toward Us is the [final] destination. (43)
يَوْمَ تَشَقَّقُ ٱلْأَرْضُ عَنْهُمْ سِرَاعًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ حَشْرٌ عَلَيْنَا يَسِيرٌ (٤٤)
The day the earth is split open for [disentombing] them, [they will come out] hastening. That mustering is easy for Us [to carry out]. (44)
نَّحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يَقُولُونَ ۖ وَمَآ أَنتَ عَلَيْهِم بِجَبَّارٍ ۖ فَذَكِّرْ بِٱلْقُرْءَانِ مَن يَخَافُ وَعِيدِ (٤٥)
We know well what they say, and you are not there to compel them. So admonish by the Quran those who fear My threat. (45)

Key Points

The term Jabbār (جبّار) means both the Compensator (or Restorer), which is one of the attributes and Names of Allah, «العزیز الجبّار المتكبّر» (54), and it also means compulsion and domination over others through transgression, which is an ugly and forbidden attribute. «ما أنت علیهم بجبّار»

In these verses, Allah states: Do not doubt the Day of Resurrection because: - Your death and life are in Our hands. «نحیى و نمیت» - For your emergence from the graves, We shall cleave the earth. «تشقق الارض سراعاً» - We shall gather the scattered human beings. «ذلك حشر» - This task is not difficult at all. «یسیر»

In Ayah 39, He said: «فاصبر على ما یقولون» (So be patient with what they say). The subsequent verses point to matters that make a person patient, including: - The remembrance and glorification of Allah. «فاصبر على ما یقولون و سبّح بحمد ربّك» - Remembering the Day of Resurrection. «فاصبر... ذلك یوم الخروج» - Attention to and awareness of Allah's presence. «فاصبر... الینا المصیر» - Allah's attention and knowledge regarding the vain talk of the opponents. «فاصبر... نحن أعلم بما یقولون»

The beginning of this Surah was dedicated to swearing by the Quran, and its end is dedicated to reminding and paying attention to the Quran.

54) Hashr, 23.

Messages

  1. No one possesses the power to give life and cause death except Allah. «انّا نحن نحیى و نمیت»

  2. Remembering that the return of all is to Him increases a person's strength in the face of difficulties. «فاصبر على ما یقولون... الینا المصیر»

  3. The duty of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله is to propagate the religion, not to impose it. «و ما أنت علیهم بجبّار» (Acceptance of religion must be voluntary.)

  4. The focus of religious propagation must be the Quran, as it is the best means of remembrance (admonition). «فذكّر بالقرآن»

  5. The sublime, logical, and innate teachings of the Quran are sufficient for people's acceptance, and there is no need for compulsion. «ما انت علیهم بجبّار فذكّر بالقرآن»

  6. The one who accepts admonition is he who fears the threat (of the Day of Judgment). «فذكّر... من یخاف وعید»

  7. The truths of religion are inherent in human nature; only a reminder is necessary. «فذكّر بالقرآن» (The word dhikr (ذكر) means reminding of hidden or forgotten things.)

  8. For the one who does not accept the Day of Resurrection, the best propagation from the best preachers will be of no benefit. «من یخاف وعید»
